question.label: Next support
question.name: What support from me would help you move faster this fortnight?
question.description: Closes on a concrete manager action for the next stretch of onboarding.

answer: If we define what good looks like for my next two tickets, I will feel much steadier.

assessment.note: Names a specific missing support need — defining what good looks like on the next two tickets — which signals current clarity and ramp support are still insufficient.

new_queue.label: Priority ranking
new_queue.name: If you had to cut your work in half tomorrow, what would you drop first — and what's non-negotiable?

issues: ref_alias q_next_support_3 not in remaining queue — treating as new

history.answer_excerpt: I can do the tasks, but I still second-guess whether my approach matches team expectations.

Pairing once or twice a week would probably prevent me from getting stuck silently.
Local setup is better now, but I still lose time when the design and API details do not match.
I am not always sure who should approve what before I push.
I usually wait too long before asking because I want to solve things myself first.
When feedback is specific and fast, I move much quicker.
I still feel fuzzy on where quality bars differ between quick fixes and core flows.
